返回本批次

OpenJDK：巴拿马

OpenJDK: Panama

openjdk.org 作者 tosh 分数 52 评论 13 2026/04/06 02:33
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 巴拿马项目：互连 JVM 和本机代码 我们正在改进和丰富 Java 虚拟机和定义良好但“外国”（非 Java）API 之间的连接，包括许多 C 程序员常用的接口。为此，巴拿马项目将包括以下大部分或全部组成部分：
  2. 从 JVM 调用本机函数 从 JVM 或 JVM 堆内部访问 JVM 堆中的新数据布局 JVM 头文件的本机元数据定义 API 提取工具 (jextract) 本机库管理 API 面向本机的解释器和运行时“挂钩”类和方法解析 “挂钩”面向本机的 JIT 优化工具或包装器插入，用于难以集成的本机库的安全探索工作
  3. 社区 该项目由 Hotspot Group 赞助。
  4. 邮件列表和新闻
  5. panama-dev — 外部函数和内存 API，矢量 API jextract-dev — jextract 工具 请继续关注 Inside.java 上最新的巴拿马开发

Original Summary

  1. Project Panama: Interconnecting JVM and native code We are improving and enriching the connections between the Java virtual machine and well-defined but “foreign” (non-Java) APIs, including many interfaces commonly used by C programmers. To this end, Project Panama will include most or all of these components:
  2. native function calling from JVM native data access from JVM or inside JVM heap new data layouts in JVM heap native metadata definition for JVM header file API extraction tools (jextract) native library management APIs native-oriented interpreter and runtime “hooks” class and method resolution “hooks” native-oriented JIT optimizations tooling or wrapper interposition for safety exploratory work with difficult-to-integrate native libraries
  3. Community This Project is sponsored by the Hotspot Group.
  4. Mailing lists & News
  5. panama-dev — Foreign Function & Memory API, Vector API jextract-dev — jextract tool Stay tuned on latest Panama development at Inside.java

评论（默认中文）

andy800 2026/04/06 08:39
巴拿马的一部分是 Vector API，目前处于“孵化”状态。 Kotlin Notebooks 是 Python 中 pandas 或 Polars（以及 R 中 dplyr）的一个很好的数据框替代品，对于相对较小的数据集工作得很好，但在处理大数据计算时确实速度较慢。载体应该可以显着缩小这一差距。
Part of Panama is the Vector API, currently in "incubation". Kotlin Notebooks are a great dataframe alternative to pandas or polars in Python (and dplyr in R), and work fine for relatively small data sets, but are indeed slower when dealing with calculations on large data. Vectors should reduce that gap significantly.
在 HN 中查看
corroclaro 2026/04/06 08:51
查看 Clojure tech.ml.dataset 库 - 它们非常非常快
Check out the Clojure tech.ml.dataset libraries - they are very very fast

真的很酷，谢谢。
Really cool, thanks.

pjmlp 2026/04/06 14:19
这是一个巨大的改进，已经可用于几个 Java 版本，但 Android Java 很可能永远不会支持。
A great improvement, already available for a few Java versions, that most likely Android Java will never support.

grg0 2026/04/06 07:44
我喜欢 Java 通过追赶其他编程语言三十年来所取得的成果来进行“创新”。
I love it how Java is "innovating" by catching up to things that other programming languages have had for three decades.
在 HN 中查看
marginalia_nu 2026/04/06 08:04
嗯，是的。 当其他语言进行实验时，等待和看看什么有效，这是该语言的明确设计哲学。
Well, yeah. It's the explicit design philosphy of the language to wait and see what works as other languages do the experimenting.

patmorgan23 2026/04/06 10:24
你为什么对他们让语言变得更好感到生气？
Why are you mad they're making the language better?

millipede 2026/04/06 08:28
还有什么语言做得更好？
What other language does it better?
在 HN 中查看
latchkey 2026/04/06 08:07
“我们正在改进和丰富 Java 虚拟机和定义良好但“外国”（非 Java）API 之间的连接，包括许多 C 程序员常用的接口。”

哪里说“创新”？

"We are improving and enriching the connections between the Java virtual machine and well-defined but “foreign” (non-Java) APIs, including many interfaces commonly used by C programmers."

Where does it say "innovating"?

在 HN 中查看
grg0 2026/04/06 08:11
他们正在语言上做一些新的事情 -> 创新。

JNI 始终是执行 FFI 的错误方式。 FFI 不应要求对本机代码进行任何更改或包装；如果做不到这一点，那就是不必要的，而且效率低下。然而，不知何故，在爪哇土地上，这在 2026 年仍然是常态？

我真的很想喜欢 Java，但是天哪，它有很多缺点，而且创新速度几乎为零。

They are doing something new in the language -> innovating.

JNI was always the wrong way to do FFI. FFI should require no changes or wrappers in the native code; anything short of that is unnecessary and inefficient. Yet, somehow, in Java land, this is still the norm in 2026?

I'd really want to love Java, but man, it has a long laundry list of warts and a near-zero pace of innovation.

在 HN 中查看
nayroclade 2026/04/06 08:44
如果您在引号中包含“创新”之类的单词，则通常意味着您是从链接中引用它的。它也可以表示讽刺，但在像 HN 这样我们讨论已发表文章的上下文中，它通常是模棱两可的。

至于 Java，我同意它在 Sun 时代的发展速度相当缓慢，但从我所看到的情况来看，自从 Oracle 收购和 Brian Goetz 成为架构师以来，Java 的发展速度显着加快。

无论 Java 有多糟糕，与 JavaScript 相比都算不了什么。仅仅添加一个新的库函数就需要十年的时间，并且每一个新的语法提案都是 DOA。

If you include a word like innovating in quotes it typically implies that you're quoting it from the link. It can also signify irony, but in a context like HN where we're discussing a published article, it's often ambiguous.

As for Java, I'd agree that its pace of advance was pretty glacial during the Sun era, but from what I've seen has picked up considerably since the Oracle acquisition and Brian Goetz became architect.

And however bad Java is, it's nothing compared to JavaScript. It takes a decade just to add new a library function, and every new syntax proposal is DOA.

在 HN 中查看
re-thc 2026/04/06 08:33
> 然而，不知何故，在爪哇土地上，这在 2026 年仍然是常态？

FFM（本文所指的）是在一些版本之前发布的。那么问题出在哪里呢？如果您的意思是第三方库使用什么 - 这是您关心的问题吗？这就像说存在遗留代码一样。

> 它有一长串的疣清单

太令人惊讶了，因为你甚至没有提到 1。

> 创新步伐接近于零

垃圾收集？中关村？

> Yet, somehow, in Java land, this is still the norm in 2026?

FFM (what this article refers to) was released some releases ago. So what is the issue? If you mean what 3rd party libraries use - is that a concern to you? That's like saying there exists legacy code.

> it has a long laundry list of warts

It's such a surprise because you haven't even mentioned 1.

> and a near-zero pace of innovation

Garbage collection? ZGC?

latchkey 2026/04/06 08:38
这并不新鲜或创新。这就是“改进和丰富”。

你试图阻止他们改进，这是不公平的。

This isn't new or innovating. This is "improving and enriching".

You're unfairly trying to hold making improvements against them.
